findings.title: Sequence-based ordering is underspecified and currently conflicts with the store contract
findings.detail: The proposal explicitly says `RunArtifactStore.list()` returns run IDs in lexicographic order, but the design/tasks only say `findRunsForChange` uses `store.list({ changeId })` plus reads, and `findLatestRun` builds on that. Without an explicit numeric sort/max based on `extractSequence`, runs like `change-10` will sort before `change-2`, which can break `findRunsForChange`, `findLatestRun`, and next-ID generation. Update the design and tasks to require numeric sequence sorting in `findRunsForChange`, max-sequence selection in `findLatestRun` and `generateRunId`, and add tests that cover double-digit run IDs.

findings.title: The plan assumes required `RunArtifactStore` semantics without explicitly validating the LocalFs implementation
findings.detail: This change makes `list({ changeId })` filtering, deterministic ordering, atomic writes, and read-after-write behavior part of the required contract, but the design/tasks do not include explicit work to verify or adjust `LocalFsRunArtifactStore` against those guarantees. Because the CLI refactor now depends on that contract, add a concrete task to confirm or update `LocalFsRunArtifactStore` and its tests (especially `list({ changeId })` prefix filtering and ordering) so the new CLI wiring is not built on unverified assumptions.
